resources:
LCLD
@android:string/ok Loadingâ€¦ Help @android:string/copy Copied @android:string/paste Accept Add @android:string/cancel Edit Enable Delete Fused GPS Cell Export succeeded Export failed Import successful Import failed Don\'t show again
Network request failed Status code Response body Exception
Commands Transport channels Settings Secure control center
Settings General FMD Server Allowed contacts OpenCelliD Appearance Import settings Export settings Logs About
Allows you to communicate with FMD via a PIN. This allows you to send commands from a phone number that is not in the allowed contacts list. Warning: The PIN is not included in the app backup. Set PIN Change PIN Enter PIN PIN The PIN must be at least 8 characters. 16 or more are recommended. The PIN must not equal one of the commands! The command used to communicate with the FMD. Empty command not allowed, using default. Select a ringtone for the \"ring\" command. Select ringtone No ringtone picker found! Lock screen message:\nWhen locking the device, this message will appear on the lock screen. Warning: The password is not included in the app backup. Warning: Deleting won\'t work because the delete password is empty. You must both enable the feature and set a password.
General Server URL: User ID: You need the user ID to find your device with FMD Server. Write it down and keep it safe. Connection status: Success Change password Log out Do you really want to log out from your FMD Server account? Delete account This will delete all data from the server! Are you sure? Unregistering and data deletion successful! Push Sending commands from FMD Server to your device requires a UnifiedPush distributor app. Distributor: %s Push URL: %s More information about push Register Register again Install Sunup The recommended UnifiedPush distributor app is Sunup. Sunup is easy set up and uses push servers from Mozilla. Connectivity check interval FMD checks the connectivity to FMD Server every X hours. Set this to 0 to disable the connectivity check. Maximum allowed time without connectivity FMD will notify you if the last successful connection to FMD Server is more than X hours ago. This has no effect if the connectivity check is disabled. Background location upload interval FMD uploads the device\'s location every X minutes. Values below 15 minutes are not recommended. Location source The source(s) from which the location data is uploaded at regular intervals in the background.\nIf none is selected, no locations are uploaded regularly. (You can still use the web interface to force an upload. This requires push.) Upload on low battery Upload the location of this device when the battery level goes low. Set password Change password Enter password Old password New password Repeat new password The password must be at least 8 characters. 16 or more are recommended. The password must not equal one of the commands! Password changed Password must not be empty. The repeated passwords do not match. Wrong password Password change failed due to a network error. Failed login attempts to FMD Server Someone tried to log in to your FMD Server account \"%s\". Due to multiple failed passwords, the account is now locked for 10 minutes. FMD Server upgrade required The FMD Server you are using has version {CURRENT}, but this app requires at least {MIN}. Controlling your device via FMD Server may not work anymore. Please upgrade your server. FMD Server connectivity lost The FMD Server at %1$s can no longer be reached. The last successful connection was at %2$s. Enable the server connectivity check FMD can regularly check whether FMD Server is still reachable. For privacy reasons this is off by default. But for availability reasons we recommend enabling it.
Server version Error getting server version At least server version {MIN} is required, but the selected server has version {CURRENT}. Continue at your own risk. FMD Server: Login With FMD Server you can send commands over the internet, from the server to the app. You can also view the device location on a map or view pictures taken by the device. For self-hosting instructions, see the website. Open FMD Server website Use the public server Server URL Login Register Privacy policy FMD ID The FMD ID identifies your account on the FMD Server. Leave this empty to let the server generate an ID. The server administrator can see the FMD ID. Password Choose a password that cannot be guessed, but that you can recover when you lose your device. The password grants access to remotely running commands on your device. The server administrator cannot access or reset your password. The FMD ID and password must not be empty. Registration token Private instances may require a secret token. Leave this empty if the instance is public. Network request failed with HTTP 401. This can happen if the server requires a registration token and the token you provided was wrong.
The contacts in this list are allowed to send FMD commands via SMS. Commands from everyone else will be ignored. No allowed contacts. Click the button to add some! Add contact Congratulations, you have added your first contact! You can now communicate with FMD by sending an SMS that starts with \"%1$s\" from the allowed phone number. For example, send \"%2$s help\" or \"%3$s flash\". When everything works, the app will respond. This contact already exists. No contact picker application found! Add phone number Name Phone number Allowed contacts reset Due to a crash, the allowed contacts were reset. Please configure them again. This phone number is invalid!
Your temporary FMD access expired. To continue using FMD, send the PIN again.
OpenCelliD API Access Token FMD can use the OpenCelliD service to get an approximate location based on the cell tower that the device is connected to. An internet connection is required for every location query. You also need to create an account at OpenCelliD and get your own API Access Token. Privacy: keep in mind that OpenCelliD learns your current cell tower and thus your location with every API query. You can contribute to OpenCelliD by collecting and uploading cell tower information. You need a dedicated app for this. One such app is Tower Collector. Download Tower Collector Open OpenCelliD website Test OpenCelliD connection No cell connection
Language Choose language System default Theme Choose theme Follow system Light Dark Dynamic colors
Logs Export logs Clear logs Do you really want to delete all log entries?
About Source code Wiki`n LCLD (Locate & Control Lost Device) developed by Pawan Washudev. \n\n❤️ HUGE THANKS to Nulide and the original Find My Device (FMD) team for their incredible open-source work which made this project possible. FMD (Locate & Control Lost Device) allows you to remotely locate your device. You can communicate with FMD over SMS, instant messengers, or using FMD Server. Locate your device, make it ring, lock it, or wipe it - all from remote.
Action required You need to update some settings to continue using FMD. Click to open. Unregistering failed Unregistering failed with the following error: {ERROR}\n\nYou can continue anyway, but your account might not have been deleted from the server. If you continue, your account will be deleted locally from this device. Continue anyway Don\'t continue
The FMD PIN was reset during an app update. To continue using FMD with a PIN, please set the PIN again.
This is the edge variant of FMD. Here, the default command keyword is \"fmde\" (instead of \"fmd\"). For example, to send the locate command, use \"fmde locate\". This allows having both edge and prod installed on the same device, and targeting commands to one of the two.
Cannot run command %1$s: Missing permission(s) %2$s.
Toggle Bluetooth on and off The device does not seem to have Bluetooth. Bluetooth turned on. Bluetooth turned off. Bluetooth is on. Bluetooth is off.
Take a picture Takes a picture of with the front/back camera and uploads it to FMD Server. Currently only uploading to FMD Server is supported. You can send this command via other transport channels, too, and the picture will be uploaded to FMD Server (if an account exists). Cannot take picture: no FMD Server account exists. The device is taking a picture and will send it to FMD Server. You can view it here soon: %s
Factory-reset the device This wipes all data. Afterwards you will no longer be able to communicate with the device. To prevent accidental and malicious usage, this command requires a password and it needs to be explicitly enabled in the settings. Cannot delete: delete command is not enabled in settings. Cannot delete: missing password. Usage: %s. Cannot delete: wrong password. Dry run successful, skipping the actual device deletion. Failed to delete device due to an exception. The device should now be deleted. Goodbye!
Flash the camera light
Toggle Location Services on and off Location Services turned on. Location Services turned off. Location Services are on. Location Services are off.
Show the help FMD Commands:
Locate the device Locate the device using one or more location providers. GPS has the highest accuracy, but may not always work. The \"fused\" provider is defined by Android, and uses a combination of sources (often WiFi or cell towers). The \"cell\" provider queries the public OpenCelliD and BeaconDB services for the surrounding cell tower locations. Cannot locate: location is turned off. GPS coordinates will be sent soon. Error gaining location from OpenCelliD. This can happen if the cell tower is not in the database.\n\nPlease try the following the link: %1$s\n\n%2$s Error gaining location from BeaconDB. This can happen if the cell tower is not in the database.\n\n%s Getting a fresh GPS location failed. Maybe due to a timeout. The requested location provider \"%s\" is disabled. Last known location No last known location. Request the latest location to force a location update.
Lock the device Lock the device and optionally show a message on the lockscreen. After this, biometric unlock is unavailable and your device password is required to unlock the device. The device should now be locked.
Make the device ring The device should now be ringing.
Change the ringer mode Note that \"silent\" also enables Do Not Disturb mode. This is expected behaviour and is defined by Android. The current ringer mode is \"%s\". The ringer mode was changed from \"%1$s\" (old) to \"%2$s\" (new).
Toggle Do Not Disturb on and off Do Not Disturb mode turned on. Do Not Disturb mode turned off.
Get network statistics Get network statistics from the device. For example, its IP address and a list of surrounding Wifi networks. Network statistics:\n\nDevice IPs:\n%1$s\n\nWifi networks:\n%2$s
Ignore battery optimizations Connect Bluetooth Camera Device admin Do Not Disturb access Location Notification access Display over other apps Post notifications SMS Unused app restrictions Write to secure settings
This application is supposed to work in the background. To ensure it works even when your device is in standby, you need to turn off battery optimization for this app. FMD will occasionally send you notifications, for example to warn you that someone is locating your device. Android pauses apps that are not opened for a few months and removes their permissions. FMD, however, is supposed to work in the background. After setting it up you rarely need to open it. Therefore you need to exempt FMD from being paused, otherwise it may not work in a few months.
Required permissions Optional permissions Grant Grant write access to secure settings This permission can only be granted with Android\'s \"pm grant\" command line tool. You can execute this command manually via the Android Debug Bridge (ADB) using a computer. You can also execute it directly on your device using root or Shizuku.\n\nUser ID: %d Grant via ADB Grant via root Grant via Shizuku Root access denied Granting via Shizuku failed
SMS Send commands to your device over SMS. E.g., text \"%s help\" to your device. FMD will reply with an SMS. Only phone numbers that are in the allowlist can send commands. To send commands from a phone number that is not in the allowlist, you have to include the PIN: \"%s mypin help\". For convenience, so that you don\'t have to re-type the PIN every time, this new phone number is added to a temporary allowlist for the next 10 minutes. SMS may incur costs for both you and the person texting you. SMS requires your SIM card to be unlocked (e.g., after a reboot).
Notification reply You can allow FMD to read your notifications to listen for commands. FMD can then reply via Android\'s built-in notification reply feature. This allows you to control FMD using a messaging application of your choice, as long as it posts notifications that can be replied to. E.g., you can send a Signal message \"%s mypin help\" to your phone. Controlling FMD via notification reply requires the FMD PIN to be set. Every message must contain the correct PIN.
FMD Server FMD Server provides a web interface to control your device, view its location, and view pictures it sent. You can self-host FMD Server or use the provided instance. Data is stored encrypted on the server, protected by your password. Anyone who knows your username and password can log in to FMD Server and control your device. FMD Server requires an internet connection. This can be a mobile network (thus the SIM needs to be unlocked) or WiFi.
In-app Send commands locally from within FMD to your device. This is useful for testing. FMD will reply with a notification. Send in-app command Command Send
Usage Notifications when FMD is receiving commands PIN usage Notifications when FMD is accessed is via the PIN FMD Server Notifications related to FMD Server Security Notifications about security updates Failures Notifications when something fails, like the connection to FMD Server In-app transport channel Notifications from the in-app transport channel Command execution Notifications for currently executing commands
Command executed The command \"%s\" was executed. The command \"%1$s\" was executed by \"%2$s\". FMD PIN used The PIN was used by this number: \"%s\". It is recommended to change the PIN. Executing command The command \"%1$s\" is being executed by \"%2$s\".
Review setup and permissions Setup warnings Recommendations Missing permissions The following permissions are needed for FMD to run. Missing UnifiedPush Without a UnifiedPush distributor app you cannot receive commands from FMD Server.
FMD\nThis device is locked!\nMessage from the owner: LockScreenMessage: Usage detected. LockScreenMessage: Back-Button pressed.
STOP RINGING
Crash report FMD has crashed! Here is the log: Send log Copy log Open issue tracker Continue without reporting
Pin accepted!
